old: 他想尽了一切办法，才智终于穷尽。
new: 他的力气已经穷，无法继续前进了。
reason: Uses compound 穷尽 instead of the headword 穷 as a standalone word (rule 7).

old: He tried every possible method, and his ingenuity was finally exhausted.
new: His strength was exhausted and he could no longer continue.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence.

old: 这条路已经走穷，只好另辟蹊径。
new: 这条路已经走穷，只好换一条路走。
reason: 另辟蹊径 uses 蹊径, which is above HSK5 vocabulary.

old: This path has been exhausted; we have no choice but to find a new way.
new: This path has been exhausted; we have no choice but to take a different route.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence.

old: 村里的水源快要穷尽，大家必须节省。
new: 村里的粮食快要穷，大家必须节省。
reason: Uses compound 穷尽 instead of the headword 穷.

old: The village's water source is nearly exhausted; everyone must conserve.
new: The village's grain is nearly exhausted; everyone must conserve.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence.

old: We are going all out to support your decision.
new: We support your decision with all our strength.
reason: 'Going all out to support' is an awkward calque of 全力支持; the phrase simply means 'fully/wholeheartedly support.'

new: 他戴的手表是人工制作的，不是机器生产的。
reason: Near-duplicate of sentence_1, which also describes an artificial lake; the scenario should differ.

old: There is an artificial lake in the park.
new: The watch he is wearing is artificially made, not machine-produced.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence.

old: 我们在公园里设置了几个椅子。
new: 工厂在门口设置了一个安全检查站。
reason: 设置 is unnatural for chairs (one places/arranges chairs, not installs them); also 个 is the wrong measure word for 椅子 (correct: 把).

old: We set up several chairs in the park.
new: The factory set up a security checkpoint at the entrance.
reason: Translation updated to match corrected sentence.

old: He extended his hand into his pocket.
new: He reached his hand into his pocket.
reason: 'Extended his hand into his pocket' is unnatural English; 'reached' is the idiomatic word here.
